title: coleus capitatus
intro: Coleus capitatus, also known as Coleus aromaticus or Coleus vettiveroides, is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Coleus capitatus include treating skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and wounds, as well as being used to alleviate respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Coleus capitatus can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil, and it is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to create various remedies, including teas, creams, and ointments.
